questions.question: What does the isAlive() method in a Thread object return?
options: A) A boolean value indicating whether the thread is alive or not
B) A priority level of the thread
C) An array of thread IDs
D) An exception if the thread is interrupted
questions.explanation: The isAlive() method in a Thread object returns a boolean value indicating whether the thread is alive or not.

questions.question: What does the join() method in a Thread object do?
options: A) It starts a new thread
B) It stops a running thread
C) It waits for a thread to finish execution
D) It sets the priority of a thread
questions.explanation: The join() method in a Thread object waits for the thread to finish execution before continuing execution of the main thread.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid Thread priority?
What happens when a thread is suspended?
options: A) It stops execution
B) It waits for a condition to be met
C) It yields control back to the scheduler
D) It throws an exception
questions.explanation: When a thread is suspended, it yields control back to the scheduler, allowing other threads to run.

questions.question: Can a thread be stopped once it has started?
options: A) Yes
B) No
C) It depends on the thread's state
D) Only if it is a daemon thread
questions.explanation: A thread can be stopped once it has started, but it can also be stopped before it has started, or it can be allowed to run to completion.
